orography: landlocked inland piedmont; no coastal water at hand
why: Region fixed at NC latitude. Landlocked, ~100 mi inside the eastern coast, low inland elevation. Continental-leaning humid subtropical: real four-season swing (2015 ran 1F to 96F).

Daidoji vassal house on the coast, 25 mi north of Reiji on the map (inferred lat 36.83) - effectively Reiji's latitude, so this is the TRUE maritime counterpart to landlocked Reiji and Virginia Beach (36.85) is a near-exact match. The harbor city of Uminari lies just north of it. Latitude derived from the Three Man Alliance map (2026-07-19): graph-paper grid measures 60 px and the legend gives one square = 5 miles, so 12 px/mile; ocean on the right fixes up = north; castle stars snapped to ink centroid and offsets taken from Shiro Reiji, the GM-established anchor, at 69 mi/degree.

orography: eastern foot of a mountain range - the range rises to the north and northeast and dies out just here at its southern terminus; Kitsune Mori lowland forest west and southwest, Sparrow Hills then ocean eastward
why: Map-derived lat 37.68 (84 mi north of Reiji). Sited at the foot of the mountains, not on them: a jokamachi is literally the 'town below the castle', and even a true mountain castle (Gifu on Mt. Kinka) kept its town on the flat at the base - so the settlement's elevation, not the height above it, is what the weather model should read. Charlottesville is the EASTERN (Piedmont) foot of the Blue Ridge, which preserves the map's orientation: mountains inland to the west and north, lowland running out to the ocean eastward. The Shenandoah Valley flank (Lexington, Buena Vista) was rejected - it would put a range between the site and the sea and impose a rain shadow the map does not support, since nothing lies west of Shinden Kitsune but lowland forest. CHOSEN FOR RECOGNISABILITY over the closer-latitude Amherst/Big Island (37.53-37.58, ~710-740 ft): Charlottesville is 0.35 deg north (~24 mi) and 230 ft lower, and those two errors nearly cancel (~+0.5F from latitude, ~-0.8F from elevation). KNOWN IMPERFECTION: the map puts Shinden Kitsune only ~35-55 mi from the sea, while Charlottesville is ~170 mi inland. Rokugan's geography is compressed - real Virginia has no mountains within 55 mi of the coast - so latitude and mountain-foot character were kept at the cost of continentality. Expect the real Shinden Kitsune to run a little milder in winter and more humid than Charlottesville suggests.

orography: coastal hilly upland - the Sparrow Hills, formerly the Iron Foothills. Rolling relief, NOT mountains: marshy valley bottoms between dry hilltops, slopes too steep to hold flooded paddies. Coastline generally low, with at least one notable sea cliff (the monastery cliff).
why: Map-derived lat 37.90, hard on the coast; Chincoteague at 37.93 is 0.03 deg off and is the recognisable choice over the near-identical Wallops Island (GM and several players have vacationed there). Sparrow lands sit directly south of Kyuden Kakita, which the map and l7r.md agree on. HILLINESS WAS TESTED AND REJECTED AS AN ANALOG CRITERION. Two effects were measured, both ~1-2F, i.e. below the noise floor of the latitude signal: (a) elevation - Atlantic Highlands NJ (213 ft, the one hilly mid-Atlantic coast) vs flat Sandy Hook 5 mi away differs by at most 0.8F, which is just the 3.5F/1000ft lapse rate; (b) barrier-island maritimity - Chincoteague vs inland Pocomoke City MD differs by 1.8F of daily swing, 3 frost days, and ~1F in Jan low / Jul high. ERA5's ~9-31 km grid cannot resolve a few hundred feet of relief anyway, so no analog choice can buy hill effects; they belong in the terrain notes below, not in the site selection. TERRAIN NOTES the analog does NOT carry, but which the Sparrow ecology in l7r.md implies and real hill-farming confirms: cold air drains downhill overnight, so the marshy taro valleys are FROST POCKETS that freeze before the slopes; the mid-slope thermal belt is the warmest night ground, which is exactly where tea and mulberry are planted (real Japanese tea is sited on slopes for this reason); the dry hilltops carrying millet and barley are windier and ~1-3F cooler than the shore. Expect the growing season to run shorter in the hollows than the coastal analog shows. KNOWN IMPERFECTIONS: Chincoteague is sand and salt marsh with no farmland and no cliffs, so it carries the CLIMATE only, not the soil, the relief, or the monastery cliff.
The empire-scale baseline anchor. The whole east-coast ladder is pinned off this point.
One notch north of Otosan Uchi/NYC on the same coast.

GM-pinned: Kyuden Isawa sits ON the coast in a forested region = Acadia. The contrast with Acadia is that Rokugan's great forest (the Isawa Woodlands) lies inland rather than running to the shore - the castle itself is coastal. Harsh snowy north - Winter Court country, where a heavy snow can trap you for months.

orography: the largest forest in the Empire; deep inland, does not reach the coast
why: Distinct from coastal Kyuden Isawa - an inland forest zone. Interior Maine: colder, snowier, more continental than the coast at the same latitude.
Crane heartland, north of Kakita on the coast.

orography: coastal barrier island, flat and sea-level
why: Seaside Crane castle 146 mi north of Reiji on the map (inferred lat 38.58), so Ocean City MD - NOT Virginia Beach, which sits at Shiro Etsuko's latitude and was previously mis-assigned here. Seats Kakita below Kyuden Doji (Atlantic City, 39.36) on the ladder. This is the coastal castle on the Three Man Alliance map (the scan reads 'Kahita' but it is Kakita). Latitude derived from the Three Man Alliance map (2026-07-19): graph-paper grid measures 60 px and the legend gives one square = 5 miles, so 12 px/mile; ocean on the right fixes up = north; castle stars snapped to ink centroid and offsets taken from Shiro Reiji, the GM-established anchor, at 69 mi/degree.
Southern Crane holding, warmer and further south than Doji/Kakita.

orography: near-peninsular; Earthquake Bay to the west (much smaller than the Gulf)
why: The southern tip of the empire. Least-exact anchor: Earthquake Bay is far smaller than the Gulf and Asahina is less peninsular than Florida, so expect slightly cooler winters / bigger range than Tampa proper.
